methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
SpectralX is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by IDF special-forces veterans. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private-stage companies including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI.

The peer-median method applied a cohort of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables. Stark Defence trades at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M, and NODA AI at $125M, yielding a median valuation of $225M and a range of $125M to $500M. This method represents the primary triangulation lens given data availability; additional methods such as DCF or revenue multiples could not be applied without revenue, margin, or growth-rate inputs.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $191M to $259M, centered on a $225M median. The relatively wide peer range ($375M spread) reflects heterogeneity in customer concentration, technology maturity, and contract pipeline within the defense-tech Series A cohort. Key uncertainties include SpectralX's revenue traction, customer diversification relative to the comp set, and defensibility of its core technology. Without disclosed fundraising metrics or financial performance, this median should be treated as indicative rather than determinative of fair value.
